Petition For Quasi-Specific Performance Of Contract To Make Will; And For Damages For Conversion, Financial Elder Abuse, And Wrongful Eviction
Off Calendar Nov-22-2011 Off Calendar

Petition For Quasi-Specific Performance Of Contract To Make Will; And For Damages For Conversion, Financial Elder Abuse, And Wrongful Eviction
Off Calendar Nov-08-2011 Continued To Nov-22-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order Approving Compromise Of Litigation
Off Calendar Nov-14-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: This matter was heard with case number PES-11-294723 Estate of Henry Santiano.

Appearances: Edward Broitman for Teresita Manjarres. John Boessenecker for Ben Gallo.

Mr. Boessenecker addressed the issue of his Attorney contingent fee agreement (litigation) not being pre-approved, and explained circumstances and services provided. The Court is approving both Petitions for Order Approving Compromise of Litigation (subject to review of the orders) but reserves the issue of fees. Mr. Boessenecker regarding of the week.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy A. Whitehead.

Petition For Quasi-Specific Performance Of Contract To Make Will; And For Damages For Conversion, Financial Elder Abuse, And Wrongful Eviction
Off Calendar Oct-11-2011 Continued To Nov-08-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Quasi-Specific Performance Of Contract To Make Will; And For Damages For Conversion, Financial Elder Abuse, And Wrongful Eviction
Off Calendar Sep-27-2011 Continued To Oct-11-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Quasi-Specific Performance Of Contract To Make Will; And For Damages For Conversion, Financial Elder Abuse, And Wrongful Eviction
Off Calendar Aug-23-2011 Continued To Sep-27-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Allowance On Account Of Compensation To Executor And To Attorney For Ordinary Services
Off Calendar Aug-08-2011 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Edward Broitman for Teresita Manjarres (Petitioner).

Mr. Broitman stated that they successfully settled the matter at mediation, that a dismissal will be filed after a disclosed amount is paid as part of the agreement and that Mr. Boessenecker will file a petition for approval of the settlement agreement prior to the August 23, 2011 hearing date. Mr. Broitman asked that 50% of the statutory fees and some of the fees in connection with the litigation be paid, and stated that the settlement agreement will reduce the value of the inventory by about 25%. The matter was taken under submission for the Court to see if some portion of the statutory fees can be paid. The Court stated that it is not inclined to award any of the extraordinary fees at this time. The Court expects a petition for approval of the settlement agreement to be filed in both the Manjarres Estate and the Santiano Estate.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

Petition For Quasi-Specific Performance Of Contract To Make Will; And For Damages For Conversion, Financial Elder Abuse, And Wrongful Eviction
Off Calendar Jul-19-2011 Continued To Aug-23-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Allowance On Account Of Compensation To Executor And To Attorney For Ordinary Services
Off Calendar Jun-28-2011 Continued To Jul-26-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Edward Broitman for Teresita Manjarres (Petitioner).

The Court noted that Ms. Harrington associated Mr. Boessenecker and that Mr. Boessenecker was not present. Judge Wiss disclosed that Counsel Amy Harrington is the Spouse of a fellow Bench Officer and that she does not feel that this fact will affect her ability to be fair and impartial to all Parties and all Counsel. Mr. Broitman stated that he did provide notice to Mr. Boessenecker and that it was his understanding that Mr. Boessenecker wasn't going to have any objections. The Court stated that it was uncertain as ton whether or not Mr. Boessenecker intended to appear and or object, that it has significant concerns about the petition, that it was not clear as to what is going to happen with this Estate or whether 50% of the work is complete and that the tentative was to deny the petition. Mr. Broitman stated that they have mediation set for July 21, 2011. The petition for allowance of compensation, etc. was continued to July 26, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

Petition For Quasi-Specific Performance Of Contract To Make Will; And For Damages For Conversion, Financial Elder Abuse, And Wrongful Eviction
Off Calendar May-17-2011 Continued To Jul-19-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Edward Broitman for Teresita Manjares (Executor). John Boessenecker for Ben Gallo (Executor of the Estate of Henry Santiano).

Judge Wiss noted that Amy Harrington filed the Creditor's Claim and disclosed that Amy Harrington is the Spouse of a fellow Bench Officer and that she does not feel that this fact will affect her ability to be fair and impartial to all Parties and all Counsel. Mr. Boessenecker presented oral argument as to why the matter should be heard in Probate rather than the Civil Department. The Parties agreed to participate in mediation. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than July 15, 2011. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by July 15, 2011. The matter was continued to, and the Parties are to return on, July 19, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Lazatin.

Petition For Quasi-Specific Performance Of Contract To Make Will; And For Damages For Conversion, Financial Elder Abuse, And Wrongful Eviction
Off Calendar May-02-2011 Continued To May-17-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
